eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: The MCC assumes 1 participation/validator-class node maintaining a pruned local ledger state. RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the framework cost model.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to the Algorand MCC; Algorand node documentation

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Representative benchmark-level Layer-1 throughput estimate for Algorand, positioned in the low-thousands TPS range. The selected value is intended to reflect Algorand's high-throughput positioning in the literature while remaining conservative relative to peak or marketing-oriented claims.
ps_throughput.source: Algorand whitepaper and official Algorand documentation

eco_variable_transaction_cost.conversion_note: Algorand's minimum transaction fee is 0.001 ALGO. The EUR value is treated here as an approximate benchmark-level low protocol fee and is aligned with the benchmark appendix representation.

eco_storage_footprint.method_note: Baseline storage footprint for the adopted MCC, assuming a pruned local ledger state rather than a full archival node.
eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework baseline storage assumption for Algorand MCC; Algorand node documentation

parallel_execution.motivation: The evaluated Algorand Layer-1 baseline does not provide native ledger-level parallel execution through partitioned transaction execution.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: The adopted baseline does not include native horizontal state partitioning or sharding.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Algorand avoids proof-of-work-style global mining competition and relies on committee-based agreement, reducing global coordination bottlenecks compared with longest-chain architectures.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Pure Proof-of-Stake with VRF-based committee selection provides a scalable coordination model that does not require all participants to communicate with all others in every consensus step.

append_only_structural_history.motivation: The Algorand ledger maintains an append-only sequence of committed blocks and transactions.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Blocks and transactions are cryptographically linked through the blockchain structure and authenticated state transitions.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions and consensus messages are authenticated through digital signatures.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Full nodes and participating consensus nodes can independently verify ledger integrity under the protocol rules.

rci_tamper_evidence_auditability.source: Algorand ledger, transaction, and consensus documentation

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Committed blocks are final under Algorand consensus and are not subject to probabilistic longest-chain reorganization.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Finality requires distributed committee-based agreement under the Pure Proof-of-Stake protocol.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: No single validator or ordinary participant can unilaterally roll back committed state under the native protocol model.

rci_finality_rollback_resistance.source: Algorand Pure Proof-of-Stake consensus and finality documentation

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.configuration_note: Score refers to Algorand's native distributed consensus model, not to the single-node MCC in isolation.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Block production and agreement do not depend on a structurally privileged permanent writer node.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: Progress depends on distributed protocol participation rather than on a single server or primary node.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: The protocol continues automatically while consensus assumptions remain satisfied, without requiring manual failover.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: The consensus process tolerates individual node failures within the fault assumptions of the protocol.

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: No single entity has unilateral authority over valid ledger state under the native Algorand protocol.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Validation and block confirmation structurally require distributed consensus participation.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Consensus participation is governed by protocol-level stake-based participation and cryptographic committee selection rather than discretionary enterprise admission.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: Consensus participation is dynamically selected under protocol rules rather than fixed to a closed validator set.

ags_control_distribution_governance.source: Algorand Pure Proof-of-Stake validator and committee-selection model

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: Algorand does not implement a native enterprise identity and privilege governance framework comparable to RBAC or consortium access-control systems.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: There is no native protocol-level identity lifecycle governance comparable to permissioned enterprise platforms.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: The protocol does not provide a native identity and access governance framework in the sense required by this criterion.

ags_identity_access_governance.source: Algorand account model and public permissionless protocol design

non_unilateral_compromise_requirement.motivation: A single participant cannot unilaterally compromise ledger validity or finality under the native protocol assumptions.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Effective compromise requires coordinated control of a large fraction of the relevant consensus participation power rather than unilateral action.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: Violation of Byzantine-agreement safety assumptions requires compromise beyond the tolerated Byzantine threshold, corresponding to a supermajority-style compromise condition under the framework.

ags_collusion_resilience.source: Algorand Byzantine Agreement and Pure Proof-of-Stake security model
